mechanism_narrative: Declining unemployment rate signals tightening labor market and rising wage pressures, which compress corporate profit margins and reduce equity valuations. This mechanism operates continuously as a trend signal predicting negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Current UNRATE at 3.4% indicates tight labor market
failure_condition: Rank IC of UNRATE momentum signal exceeds 0.05 in absolute value over any 2-year rolling window, or negative 20-day returns do not occur in >55% of instances when UNRATE declines month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate above 4.0% signals restrictive monetary policy that raises discount rates for future corporate earnings. When FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.25%, equity risk premiums expand and S&P 500 forward returns turn negative over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS > 4.25%, S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in >50% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth and retail sales jointly signal aggregate demand strength. When both GDPC1 growth and RSAFS momentum are positive, equity earnings expectations rise, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days. This interaction-driven mechanism requires simultaneous strength in both series.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 20198.091 and RSAFS at 696982.0 suggest demand resilience
failure_condition: Interaction term (GDPC1 × RSAFS momentum) produces Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns are not significantly higher when both series are positive versus baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production index below 103.0 signals supply-side weakness and potential stagflation risk. When INDPRO declines or stagnates, equity valuations compress due to earnings uncertainty and rising cost pressures, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: When INDPRO < 103.0 and declining, S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in >55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) and payroll employment (PAYEMS) jointly indicate construction sector and labor market health. When both series show month-over-month growth, forward demand for materials and services strengthens, supporting positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days through earnings growth expectations.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1382.0 and PAYEMS at 155073.0 indicate construction and employment strength
failure_condition: Joint momentum signal (HOUST growth AND PAYEMS growth) produces Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are not significantly higher when both series grow versus baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Inflation measured by CPIAUCSL above 300.0 combined with FEDFUNDS above 4.0% signals the Federal Reserve maintaining restrictive policy to combat persistent price pressures. This regime produces negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as real discount rates remain elevated and growth expectations decline.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 300.536 and FEDFUNDS at 4.33% both exceed thresholds
failure_condition: When both CPIAUCSL > 300.0 and FEDFUNDS > 4.0%, S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in >55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment growth (PAYEMS) accelerates above 150,000 persons month-over-month, signaling robust labor demand that sustains consumer spending and corporate earnings growth. This always-on trend mechanism predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Current PAYEMS at 155073.0 indicates strong employment level
regime_hint: any regime with positive labor market momentum
failure_condition: Rank IC of PAYEMS momentum signal falls below 0.03 in absolute value over any 2-year rolling window, or positive 5-day returns do not occur in >55% of instances when PAYEMS grows month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) and payroll employment (PAYEMS) jointly measure productive capacity utilization. When both series decline simultaneously, it signals demand destruction and recession risk, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as equity risk premiums expand.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declining AND PAYEMS declining month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:Both series currently near recent highs, but mechanism activates on joint decline
failure_condition: When both INDPRO and PAYEMS decline simultaneously, S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in >50% of triggered instances, or Rank IC of joint decline signal < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.
